description: A Sustainability Specialist is conducting a long-term climate risk assessment for a proposed 50 MW solar farm in Phoenix, Arizona. Quantify how much a +3°C global warming scenario reduces the plant's annual energy yield compared to the historical baseline data found at /home/ga/SAM_Weather_Data/phoenix_az_tmy.csv. Create a modified weather file at /home/ga/SAM_Weather_Data/phoenix_plus_3c.csv as an exact structural copy of the baseline with 3.0 added to every hourly ambient temperature value, ensuring the original file remains unchanged. Model a 50,000 kW DC system using the PySAM Pvwattsv8 module for both scenarios with these specifications: Module Type 0, Array Type 2 (1-axis tracking), Tilt 0, Azimuth 180, System Losses 14.08%, DC-to-AC Ratio 1.2, and Inverter Efficiency 96%. Save a JSON report to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/climate_risk_report.json with the keys baseline_energy_kwh, warming_scenario_energy_kwh, energy_loss_kwh, and percentage_loss_pct.
